balance_note: attempt_1 reported available 1007; attempt_2 reported available 849. The ~158-credit drop is NOT from the enrichment (it was refused) — the only intervening successful call was a companies/search, so searches DO consume credits. Attempt_1 was the async path (failed at progress 100); attempt_2 was the synchronous pre-flight 402. The 'no credits deducted' guarantee applies cleanly to the synchronous 402 (refused before execution), not necessarily to the async failed path.
finding: Enrichment of this contact requires ~2,740 credits; org balance was ~849-1,007. Reducing requested fields (phone+signals -> email only) barely changed the required cost (2740 -> 2736), so cost is driven by identifier RESOLUTION, not the field set. The synchronous 402 was returned BEFORE execution and charged nothing (matches documented behavior: '402 means insufficient credits and no credits are deducted'). This is the budget-control behavior the agent-interface-contract argues for: typed error + exact required/available numbers + no silent overspend, so an agent can stop gracefully.
